summary: Executed backend pytest regression and frontend Playwright checks against preview URL. Core APIs and UI flows work, with one backend contract mismatch.

critical.issue: Response uses plain_english_explanation instead of required explanation field in requested contract.

action_items: Align /api/risks/top contract with requested field name explanation (or document compatibility mapping).
Re-run backend regression after contract fix.
critical_code_review_comments: API contract drift: backend model field plain_english_explanation differs from review requirement explanation.
CORS is configured with allow_credentials=true and wildcard origins default; this can cause credentialed cross-origin constraints in strict browser scenarios.
seed_data_creation: Uploaded temporary in-memory browser test files (assets/vulnerabilities/threat_intelligence/business_services/synthetic_threat_report) and then reset using /api/data/sample.
context_for_next_testing_agent: NIST sync succeeded (1189 controls), dashboard rendered top 5 cards, sync button success banner appeared, upload accepts multiple .csv/.md via hidden input set_input_files. Backend pytest failing only on explanation field contract.
rca of the issue: Repro: run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_cyber_risk_api.py. Failure occurs in test_top_5_risks_payload_and_guidance because API payload contains plain_english_explanation while expected contract requested explanation. Mitigation: add explanation alias in RiskEntry response (or rename field) and keep frontend backward-compatible during transition.
